Joint Ntc Of Motion To Strike Portions Of Pltfs Second Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Nov-20-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS CHARLES LOUCKS AND VERNON LOUCKS' JOINT MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFF'S SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT.
Appearances: Geoffrey Spellberg of the Spellberg Law Offices (837.0456) appearing for plaintiff and responding party; Terry Gross of Gross & Belsky LLP (544.0200) for defendant and moving party.
Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: the motion to strike is granted without leave to amend as to paragraph 26 of the 2nd AC. The allegation that defendant Vernon Loucks released his ability to execute on his loans contradicts the carve-out language of the release, which is attached to the complaint. The motion is denied as to paragraph 27. Paragraph 2.6 is reasonably susceptible to plaintiff's interpretation that Vernon Loucks cannot act as Comdisco's agent and the Court cannot resolve that issue on this motion to strike. See Aragon-Haas v. Family Security Ins. Services, Inc., (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 232, 239 ("While plaintiffs' interpretation of the contract may prove invalid, it [is] improper to resolve the issue against her solely on her own pleading.") The Court strikes without leave to amend only that portion of paragraph 28 that refers to the settlement agreement. The Court denies defendant's request to strike the prayer for permanent injunctive relief. Order signed. (302/EHG) Judge: Ernest Goldsmith; Clerk: E. Larnauti; Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077
